title: SKILL: Network Reconnaissance
note: Goal: develop enough hands-on understanding of Nmap, Netdiscover, and Wireshark to identify where each tool fails the user, then produce a build document for a unified recon GUI that surfaces the right data without requiring command memorization.

title: host discovery fundamentals
note: nmap -sn ping sweeps, netdiscover ARP scanning. Know when each works and why ARP wins on local segments.

title: port scanning techniques
note: TCP SYN (-sS), connect (-sT), UDP (-sU), and stealth tradeoffs. Timing templates -T1 through -T5.

title: service and version detection
note: nmap -sV and -A flags. Reading banner grabs. Understanding what version info reveals about attack surface.

title: OS fingerprinting
note: nmap -O and TTL analysis. Passive fingerprinting via p0f. Why this matters for exploit selection.

title: Wireshark packet analysis
note: Capture filters vs display filters. Protocol dissection. Following TCP streams. Identifying credentials in cleartext.

title: NSE scripting engine
note: nmap --script categories: auth, vuln, brute, discovery. Running targeted scripts vs full category scans.

title: network topology mapping
note: Traceroute, TTL analysis, identifying routers and segment boundaries. Drawing the network picture.

title: passive reconnaissance
note: Shodan, Censys, theHarvester, OSINT before active scanning. Legal and operational considerations.

title: scan output formats
note: nmap -oN, -oX, -oG output. Parsing grepable output. Feeding results into other tools.

title: firewall and IDS evasion basics
note: Fragmentation, decoy scans, timing adjustments. Understanding detection thresholds.

title: scan result validation
note: Are open ports actually responding? Confirm with manual nc or telnet connection before acting on results.

title: false positive identification
note: UDP scans produce many false positives. Verify with -sV. Understand why filtered differs from closed.

title: Wireshark filter syntax
note: Test display filters on known traffic before using in live capture. Common mistakes: = vs == and port vs tcp.port.

title: nmap timing calibration
note: Too fast loses accuracy. Too slow misses time-sensitive services. Benchmark -T3 vs -T4 on your lab.

title: reference: nmap book online
note: nmap.org/book/toc.html - Gordon Lyon's full reference. Chapter 4 covers port scanning in depth.

title: Wireshark sample captures
note: wiki.wireshark.org/SampleCaptures - known-good pcap files for filter practice without live traffic.

title: lab network isolation
note: Use a dedicated virtual network. Do not scan production or public networks without authorization.

title: legal authorization documentation
note: Document scope explicitly. CTF platforms and home lab VMs satisfy this. No assumptions.

title: Wireshark capture permissions
note: Linux requires root or cap_net_raw. Windows needs WinPcap or npcap installed. Verify before sessions.

title: test target setup
note: Spin up Metasploitable2 or VulnHub VMs as scan targets. Known vulnerability sets make validation easy.

title: Wireshark pcap storage
note: Large captures fill disk fast. Set file size limits and rotation in capture options before long sessions.

title: nmap on Windows path issues
note: Add nmap to PATH. Confirm npcap version compatibility. Test with nmap --version before first session.

title: session 1: nmap baseline
note: Scan Metasploitable2 with default flags. Then -sV. Then -A. Compare output depth. Document flag effects.

title: session 2: timing and stealth
note: Same target with -T1 through -T4. Record scan duration and port discovery accuracy at each timing level.

title: session 3: NSE scripts
note: Run vuln category against Metasploitable2. Document each finding. Cross-reference with known CVEs.

title: session 4: Wireshark capture
note: Capture a full nmap -A scan in Wireshark. Identify SYN packets, RST responses, banner exchanges.

title: session 5: passive recon sprint
note: Use Shodan and theHarvester on a domain you own. Document what is publicly visible without touching the target.

title: session 6: topology mapping
note: Trace routes to 5 different targets inside the lab network. Draw the segment map. Identify gateway hops.

title: session 7: output parsing
note: Export nmap results in all three formats. Write a simple grep pipeline to extract open ports and service versions.

title: session 8: GUI build document sprint
note: Using pain points from sessions 1-7, write the build document for the unified recon GUI. Define scan workflow, results display, and export UX.
